behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: A unit test of the splitter alone can pass while the real PostToolUse classification path still feeds a single unsplit block. The failure boundary is local module handoff (event payload -> command normalization/segmentation -> trigger classifier), with no required external network/OS dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a realistic PostToolUse payload containing a multi-line shell block into the same entrypoint used by the hook, then assert the produced internal segments are per-statement (assignments and `gh ...` lines separated) rather than one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: The real risk is combined behavior across segmentation + trigger classification rules. Mocking one side can hide the regression that caused `not_a_trigger`. No real external API call or subprocess is needed to detect this classification failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the PostToolUse hook logic end-to-end in-process on input like `BRANCH=...\nTITLE=...\ngh pr create ...`; assert classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`) and includes trigger metadata expected by downstream review-loop logic.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is a negative classification boundary that depends on the same multi-module parsing/classification path as behavior 2. Unit checks on simple string matching can miss parser-context bugs; real external infra is unnecessary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook entrypoint with a multiline block ending in `gh issue create ...`; assert result is not PR-create trigger and does not open PR review-loop state.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Failure can occur in local wiring between trigger detection, identifier extraction/reconstruction, and state persistence. Mocking filesystem or persistence adapters can hide real serialization/path/key-shape bugs; use real local temp filesystem.
behaviors.test_description: Using a temp directory, invoke PostToolUse with a detected PR-create event payload and assert a state artifact is written containing the expected PR identifier fields/values and schema required by the review loop.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module contract test (PostToolUse writer -> state file -> PreToolUse reader/enforcer). A unit test on either side can miss compatibility mismatches. It stays local (modules + filesystem), so Integration is the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: In one test, run PostToolUse logic to create review-gate state, then run PreToolUse enforcement logic against the same temp state path; assert enforcement activates (e.g., blocks or requires review) based on that state without manual stubbing of serialization.
